Items.Title: Roll Call
Invocation
Items.AgendaNote:
INVOCATION - Delivered by: Reverend Doctor C. Vernon Mason, Minister in Residence, Friendship Baptist Church, 144 West 131 Street, New York, NY 10027.

Motion to spread the Invocation in full upon the record by Council Member Salaam.
Items.MinutesNote:
The Invocation was delivered by Reverend Doctor C. Vernon Mason, Minister in Residence, Friendship Baptist Church, 144 West 131 Street, New York, N.Y. 10027.

Council Member Salaam moved to spread the Invocation in full upon the Record.

Items.Title: Petitions and Communications
Land Use Call-Ups

By Council Member Epstein:
Pursuant to Rule 11.20(b) of the Council Rules and Section 19-160.2 of the New York City Administrative Code, the Council resolves that the action of the Department of Transportation approving a sidewalk café located at 93 Macdougal Street, New York, NY 10012, Borough of Manhattan, Council District 2, Community District 2, related to Application No. D 2650094566 SWM (Denino’s) shall be subject to review by the Council.
Items.MatterName: Zoning, Denino's, Manhattan (D 2650094566 SWM).

INTRODUCTION AND READING OF BILLS

Pursuant to Section 33-a of the New York City Charter, the New York City Council may vote on the following items no earlier than 30 days from the date of this notice. The New York City Council reserves the right to vote without the notice required pursuant to Section 33-a on any proposed local laws that do not relate to the public safety operations of the New York City Police Department, the Fire Department or the Department of Correction.
Items.AgendaNote: INTRODUCTION AND READING OF BILLS - Pursuant to Section 33-a of the New York City Charter, the New York City Council may vote on the following items no earlier than 30 days from the date of this notice. The New York City Council reserves the right to vote without the notice required pursuant to Section 33-a on any proposed local laws that do not relate to the public safety operations of the New York City Police Department, the Fire Department or the Department of Correction.

Items.MinutesNote:
Pursuant to Rule 8.60 of the Council, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) called for a voice vote.  Hearing those in favor, the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) declared the Resolution to be adopted.

Adopted by the Council by voice-vote.
Items.ActionText: A motion was made that this Resolution be Approved, by Council approved by voice vote.
Items.MatterName: Endangered Species Act.

Items.MinutesNote:
The presence of a quorum was announced by the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu).

There were 49 Council Members marked present at this Stated Meeting.

There is one vacant seat on the Council pending the swearing-in of the certified winner of a Special Election to be held on Tuesday, April 28, 2026 in the 3rd Council District (Manhattan).
The Majority Leader (Council Member Abreu) presiding as the President Pro Tempore.
At this point the Speaker (Council Member Menin) made the following announcements:

Whereupon on motion of the Speaker (Council Member Menin), the Majority Leader and Acting President Pro Tempore (Council Member Abreu) adjourned these proceedings to meet again for the Stated Meeting of Thursday, April 30, 2026.



MICHAEL McSWEENEY, City Clerk
